levels.name: Incitement
levels.description: Speech directly prohibited under international law. Increases risk of mass violence against a group.

call_to_violence.name: Call to Violence
call_to_violence.description: Explicit or implicit call for physical harm against a group. May be framed as defensive necessity or historical justice.

dehumanization.name: Dehumanization
dehumanization.description: Stripping human qualities from a group by comparing them to animals, insects, diseases, or objects. Makes violence or exclusion seem acceptable.

demonization.name: Demonization
demonization.description: Portraying a group as absolute evil with no redeeming qualities. Often uses words like 'regime', 'terrorists', 'enemies of the people'.

existential_threat_accusation.name: Existential Threat Accusation
existential_threat_accusation.description: Framing a group as planning genocide, civilizational destruction, or demographic replacement. Creates perceived justification for preemptive action.

levels.name: Toxification
levels.description: Speech that poisons perception of a group without explicit incitement. Creates social conditions that enable violence over time.

scapegoating.name: Scapegoating
scapegoating.description: Blaming a specific group for complex social, economic, or political problems. Redirects anger and avoids systemic analysis.

us_vs_them.name: Us vs. Them
us_vs_them.description: Framing events as a conflict between a virtuous in-group and a threatening out-group. Suppresses internal diversity and complexity.

appeal_to_fear.name: Appeal to Fear
appeal_to_fear.description: Exaggerating threats posed by a group without evidence to provoke fear-based reactions. Often uses urgent or catastrophic language.

conspiracy_targeting.name: Conspiracy Targeting
conspiracy_targeting.description: Attributing coordinated hidden malicious intent to a specific group without evidence. Example: ethnic or religious group framed as secretly controlling institutions or planning demographic replacement.

levels.name: Rhetorical Manipulation
levels.description: Structural patterns that distort reasoning and epistemic judgment. Degrade public discourse quality and lower resistance to higher-level manipulation.

false_dilemma.name: False Dilemma
false_dilemma.description: Presenting only two options when more exist. Forces the audience to choose sides and hides nuance or middle ground.

whataboutism.name: Whataboutism
whataboutism.description: Deflecting criticism by pointing to unrelated wrongdoing elsewhere. Avoids accountability without refuting the original claim.

emotional_manipulation.name: Emotional Manipulation
emotional_manipulation.description: Using emotionally charged language, imagery, or anecdotes instead of facts and evidence to drive conclusions.

group_discrediting.name: Group Discrediting
group_discrediting.description: Attacking the credibility, motives, or legitimacy of an entire group rather than addressing their arguments. Distinct from legitimate criticism of individuals.
